description: Rich, fruity and soft, this wine has a tang of orange peel along with ripe citrus and red fruits. Fresh and crisp at the end, it is ready to drink.
title: Yves Leccia 2015 Domaine D'E Croce Rosé (Patrimonio)

description: Heady tropical notes of guava and melon perfume this light-footed wine. Semidry in style, it's not deeply penetrating in fruit but offers refreshing citrus flavors and a lip-smacking honey finish. Drink now through 2019.
title: Red Newt Cellars 2015 Circle Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: This vibrant, savory white opens with alluring scents of pear, tropical fruit and a whiff of fragrant white flower. The round, medium-bodied palate shows white peach, golden apple and mineral. Fresh acidity brightens the creamy flavors while an almond note backs up the finish.
title: Selva Capuzza 2015 San Vigilio  (Lugana)

description: This linear white opens with aromas of crisp green apple, white pepper and Alpine wild flower that carry over to the bright palate along with juicy citrus notes. It's nicely balanced, with lively acidity that leaves the mouth feeling fresh and clean.
title: Abbazia di Novacella 2015 Grüner Veltliner (Alto Adige Valle Isarco)

description: Full bodied and ripe to the point of softness, this wine offers plummy fruit alongside hints of leather, mocha and dried fruit. It's soft and supple on the finish, a cuddly wine for a cool autumn evening. Drink now.
title: Bieler Père et Fils 2015 La Jassine Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

description: This opens with aromas of black-skinned berry, licorice and spice. The savory palate delivers black cherry, almond and a touch of anise set against pliant tannins. Enjoy soon.
title: Brandini 2015 Filari Lunghi  (Dolcetto d'Alba)

description: Apple and peach aromas are basic but clean and fresh. So is the palate on this Chardonnay-Sauvignon Blanc blend. Flavors of nectarine and apple don't stray far from basic, while green apple and citrus flavors are propped up on the finish by racy acidity.
title: Vicente Gandia 2015 El Miracle 120 Chardonnay-Sauvignon Blanc (Valencia)

description: A very light shade of pink, this wine is quite delicate, maybe too much so, showing light strawberry, bubblegum, nectarine and tangerine-peel notes on the nose. The palate offers a lot of texture but not a ton of flavor, with raspberry and dried-lemon elements.
title: Croma Vera 2015 Rosé (Paso Robles)

description: A blend of 58% Sauvignon Blanc and 42% Gewürztraminer, this wine was given no oak aging and has a slight percentage of residual sugar. Peach colored, it's also flavorful in peach and apricot, simple and easy to like, with a slight spritz to the end.
title: Pedroncelli 2015 Friends White (Sonoma County)

description: This is a limited-production, food-friendly wine, spicy and slightly oxidative. That gives the wine character as well as a toasty edge that contributes to its complexity. Drink this wine now.
title: Campolargo 2015 Verdelho (Bairrada)

description: The winery's value bottling, this was vinified in a mix of neutral oak and stainless steel. Enjoy soon for its pretty mix of savory herb, black cherry and molasses flavors.
title: Fullerton 2015 Three Otters Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: This Jean-Pierre and Philippe Rivière label benefits from the ripeness of the vintage to bring out red plum and a rich structure. The acidity and tannins perfectly emphasize the generous fruitiness. Drink from 2018.
title: Château de Garros 2015  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: This is a perfumed juicy wine with attractive acidity and plenty of ripe berry fruits. It has light tannins, just enough to give the wine its shape and tannic edge. Drink from 2018.
title: Château Haut-La-Péreyre 2015  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: From vines in the heart of the Entre-deux-Mers, this 85% Merlot wine is rich with layers of young black fruit and spicy wood flavors. It is still a young, structured wine and needs time for the powerful tannins to soften. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Jean de Bel Air 2015  Bordeaux

description: The wine has some extraction that leaves a bitter edge. That needs to blow off to allow the underlying ripe blackberry fruit to come through. So wait until 2018.
title: Château Labatut 2015 Grande Réserve  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: The aromas are unusual, with notes of mandarin orange, apricot, tin and herb. The palate is sweet and fleshy, showing a brightness of fruit flavor.
title: Coyote Canyon 2015 Coyote Canyon Vineyard Roussanne (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: This branded Bordeaux is packed both with fruit and with a firm structure. It has a dense texture and solid tannins although the generous fruit will allow it to age relatively quickly. Drink from 2018.
title: Fleur de Charme 2015  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: Scents of smoke, forest floor and leather lend rustic elegance to this bright briskly concentrated wine. Blackberry and cherry flavors are pleasantly crisp, framed by soft ripe tannins and an earthy lingering finish.
title: Fox Run 2015 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: This wine has light citrus aromas and flavors of grapefruit, tangerine and yellow peach. Pleasant fruit sweetness marks the finish.
title: Jidvei 2015 Demi-Sec Pinot Grigio (Jidvei)

description: A vibrant crimson in color, this offers more restrained aromas and flavors than expected. Cherries and citrus pith marry with notes of wet stone, finishing on a refreshing note despite the wine's ample weight.
title: Goose Bay 2015 Pinot Noir Rosé (South Island)

description: Leafy minty blueberry aromas announce this jammy Monastrell. There's no oak on this, nor much give to a linear flat palate. Blueberry flavors hold onto the nose's grassy herbal essence, while the finish tastes peppery and vaguely green.
title: Telmo Rodríguez 2015 Al-muvedre Monastrell (Alicante)

description: This is a soft ready-to-drink wine. It has light tannins, a mineral edge and stalky black-currant fruits. It is attractively accessible now.
title: Quinta da Rede 2015 Red (Douro)

description: Produced by the winemaking team of Casa Santos Lima in Lisboa, this wine has rich black fruits and a soft texture. Its depth, concentration and richness are finely integrated—full and fruity with a core of firmer tannins. Drink now.
title: Quinta de Porrais 2015 Sete Vales Red (Douro)

description: The nose is still shy and closed. Likewise, the body is taut and will take time to unfurl, but all the structure and promise is already here. This wine is firm and bright, slowly showing dense blueberry notes edged with cinnamon and pervaded by freshness. The structure is firm and still crunchy but with a juicy release. On the finish, white pepper shimmers in the blueberry fruit. Drink 2019–2029.
title: Prieler 2015 Johanneshöhe Blaufränkisch (Burgenland)

description: While a minuscule amount of this was made, it bears remembering the producer for this is an impressive burst of tangy citrus, lemon and orange. Light and bright, it has a pithy texture of cranberry and raspberry.
title: Belharra 2015 Rosé of Syrah (Carneros)

description: A blend of vineyards including the winery's Mill Station site, this white shines in steely acidity and layers of lemon, lime and grapefruit flavor. Tart and tangy with a light-handed approach to ripeness, it doesn't hold back on complexity, offering subtle vanilla tones on the finish.
title: Martin Ray 2015 Limited Release Chardonnay (Green Valley)
